when_to_use: riff-driven jazz-funk/fusion vamp, tight backbeat, builds and breakdowns, urgency->release arc, 96-124 BPM

defaults.feel: tight 16th funk, hard backbeat, locked to the riff

signature_moves: static vamp: one dominant/sus chord held while bass+drums drive the riff
modal interchange stab: Em9 -> Ebmaj7#11 -> Em9 (chromatic upper-structure hit)
sus-to-dom release: Gsus13 -> G13 at the section's arrival
quartal upper-structure over a pedal bass
cadence_options: vamp turnaround: Em9 -> A13 -> back to the riff
breakdown then release: strip to bass riff, then full band hits the downbeat together
no functional cadence - the release is dynamic (band re-enters), not harmonic
avoid: letting harmony wander - here harmony is subservient to the groove (see style-cheatsheets Jazz-funk/fusion)
a laid-back drag - this pocket is on the grid; do not borrow neo-soul lay-back (see ../references/cliche-register.md)

hook_archetypes.name: unison-riff
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a syncopated 16th riff hit by bass and lead (or keys) in unison/octaves
hook_archetypes.contour: an angular gesture around the root and b7, snapping back to the root on the '1'
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Intro, bass alone, then band joins in unison

hook_archetypes.name: stab-hook
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a full-band accent stab on an off-beat that punctuates the vamp
hook_archetypes.contour: an upper-structure chord hit (Ebmaj7#11 over E) as a splash of color
hook_archetypes.first_appears: the first build, marking the section change

urgency.contour: angular, tight around root/b7, insistent repetition
urgency.placement: on the grid, forward and tight

build.note_lengths: phrases climb and lengthen; add register
build.contour: reach higher each pass, tension rising over the static vamp

peak.note_lengths: held high notes / screaming lead over the fullest vamp
peak.contour: sustain the top tension tone; the band is at full drive

release.note_lengths: open up - space returns after the breakdown/hit
release.contour: resolve down to the root, let the riff settle
release.placement: on the grid, relaxing the push

drum_skeleton.low_energy: tight kick locked to the bass riff, hard snare on 2 and 4 on the grid, steady 16th hats
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: busier kick syncopation doubling the riff, ghost snares between backbeats, hat opening on accents
drum_skeleton.high_energy: full 16th drive, snare accents and fills, big band hits punctuating the vamp; a breakdown drop then a downbeat re-entry

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: bass riff states the vamp first; drums lock in; build by adding layers, release by stripping to the bass riff (breakdown) then re-entering full band on a downbeat hit

anti_boredom_rules: the vamp must build and break down - it cannot stay one static texture the whole tune
include at least one breakdown (strip to bass riff or fewer layers) followed by a full-band release hit
the riff hook must recur, varied by register, layering, or an upper-structure stab - never dead-identical for the whole tune
velocity is never flat across a voice within a bar (ghost notes low, riff accents high)
harmony stays subservient to groove, but use at least one signature_move stab so it is not one unchanging chord
